name: Central Asian Steppe
description: Kazakh steppe and surrounding grasslands, from the Caspian to the Altai

ethnic_composition.note: Scythians spoke an Iranian language (Eastern Iranian branch)

periods.note: Xiongnu Empire — dominant steppe power; forced Han China to pay tribute

ethnic_composition.presence: dominant; Xiongnu ethnicity debated (proto-Turkic or Mongolic)

periods.note: Hunnic Empire — Attila's confederation devastated Roman Empire and Germanic tribes

ethnic_composition.presence: ruling dominant; Gothic, Germanic subject peoples

periods.note: Göktürk Khaganate (First and Second) — first explicitly Turkic empire

ethnic_composition.note: Göktürk runic script; ancestor of all Turkic political traditions

periods.note: Uyghur Khaganate — Turkic successor; adopted Manichaeism (unique case)
Kimek-Kipchak confederation — Turkic nomads dominating Kazakh steppe

ethnic_composition.note: Mongols absorbed far larger Turkic populations; armies became mostly Turkic

periods.note: Golden Horde — Mongol-Turkic khanate; ruled Russian princes; gradually Turkicized
Kazakh Khanate — formation of Kazakh ethnic identity on the steppe

ethnic_composition.presence: dominant; Kazakh identity emerged from Uzbek-Kazakh split
